name: Adelaide
summary: Adelaide is the capital of South Australia, sitting between green hills and a long sandy coast. It is famous for its parklands, its grand old churches, and Adelaide Zoo, the only place in the southern hemisphere with giant pandas.
funFacts: Adelaide is nicknamed the 'City of Churches' because early settlers built dozens of beautiful church steeples that still poke above the rooftops today.
The whole city centre is wrapped in a ring of green parklands, so wherever you walk you are never far from grass, big trees and picnic spots.
Adelaide Zoo is the only zoo in the southern hemisphere with giant pandas — fluffy black-and-white bears that munch bamboo for hours every day.
Adelaide Oval is one of the world's prettiest cricket grounds, with grassy hills, an old scoreboard from 1911, and seats for 53,000 fans.
The land Adelaide is built on is called Tarndanya by the Kaurna people, who have looked after it for tens of thousands of years.
thingsToDo: Visit Adelaide Zoo to meet the giant pandas Xing Qiu and Yi Lan, plus koalas, meerkats and tree kangaroos.
Catch the historic Glenelg tram from the city out to Glenelg Beach for sandcastles and a paddle in the sea.
Wander the Adelaide Botanic Garden and step inside the giant glasshouses to find rainforest plants and water lilies.
Look around the South Australian Museum and gawp at its 18-metre sperm whale skeleton hanging from the ceiling.
Watch a cricket or footy match at Adelaide Oval, or climb its roof for a giant view across the city.
Taste fresh fruit, cheese and doughnuts at the buzzing Adelaide Central Market, one of the biggest fresh markets in the world.

wildlife.name: Koala
wildlife.description: Sleepy grey marsupial that hugs gum trees in the Adelaide Hills and munches eucalyptus leaves all day.

wildlife.name: Eastern Grey Kangaroo
wildlife.description: Big bouncy animal with a long tail and a pouch where its little joey rides.

wildlife.name: Giant Panda
wildlife.description: Famous black-and-white bear from China that lives at Adelaide Zoo and eats bamboo.

wildlife.name: Australian Sea Lion
wildlife.description: Friendly sandy-coloured seal that flops on South Australia's beaches, like Seal Bay on Kangaroo Island.

localWord.meaning: Hello, are you good? — a friendly Kaurna greeting from the Adelaide Plains.

images.alt: Adelaide's city skyline rises behind the green parklands and the River Torrens.
The big lit-up Adelaide Oval cricket stadium glows at night beside the river footbridge.
St Peter's Cathedral, a tall stone church with twin spires, in central Adelaide.
Glenelg Beach with golden sand, blue water and the long jetty stretching out to sea.
The pretty old glass Palm House sits among lawns in the Adelaide Botanic Garden.
The grand sandstone facade of Adelaide Railway Station on North Terrace.
A fluffy koala clings to a tree branch at Cleland Wildlife Park near Adelaide.
An eastern grey kangaroo standing in the bush, alert with its big ears up.
Wang Wang the giant panda munching on a stalk of bamboo at Adelaide Zoo.
An Australian sea lion and her pup resting on the sand at Seal Bay, Kangaroo Island.
